libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Keep or add JVM/native-image test configuration equivalent to `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED` when exercising interceptor, proxy, or CGLIB-backed Guice features. Prefer public Guice APIs such as `Guice.createInjector`, `AbstractModule`, bindings, injection points, providers, scopes, and method interceptors. Avoid importing old unprefixed internal classes like `com.google.inject.internal.util.MapMaker` or `com.google.inject.internal.cglib.proxy.MethodProxy`; in this artifact many shaded internals are stored with `$`-prefixed class names and direct internal imports caused the javac failure.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8858 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.sonatype.sisu:sisu-guice:2.9.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Tests that only cover simple field/constructor injection may pass without `--add-opens`, but they will miss important dynamic proxy and interceptor behavior.
Optional `org.slf4j:slf4j-api` is declared by the artifact but is not needed for normal meaningful Guice coverage unless tests intentionally target logging-specific behavior.
Direct tests of relocated internal implementation classes are brittle across sisu-guice versions and can fail at javac before reaching metadata validation.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: sisu-guice 2.9.1 is self-contained for normal Guice usage through its transitive javax.inject and aopalliance dependencies, but meaningful coverage of proxy/AOP/CGLIB paths needs java.base/java.lang opened on modern JDKs.
